expectations.text: Gives immediate containment guidance: isolate the affected SWIFT-connected server / disconnect from the SWIFT environment while preserving forensic evidence (disk images, memory captures, logs)
expectations.evidence: "Contain the threat — isolate the affected server(s) from the network (including from the SWIFT secure zone, jump servers, HSMs/tokens, and messaging interfaces) without destroying volatile evidence. Do not simply wipe/reimage before evidence capture."

expectations.text: Advises promptly notifying SWIFT of the security incident and engaging counterparty/correspondent banks immediately to attempt recall or stop of suspected fraudulent payments
expectations.evidence: "you are expected to notify your SWIFT Customer Support / Financial Crime Team as soon as possible — SWIFT's guidance is generally within 24 hours" and "Notify immediately (within hours) — request they place a hold on any suspect payment messages... and attempt recall via MT192/MT292 or gpi Stop and Recall"

expectations.text: Mentions notifying law enforcement and/or the local regulator as applicable, alongside internal escalation to senior management
expectations.evidence: "Your home regulator/central bank... typically require notification", "Financial Intelligence Unit / law enforcement — suspected fraud typically triggers a Suspicious Activity Report (SAR)", and "Board/senior management and internal audit should be notified per your incident severity matrix, typically within 24 hours"

expectations.text: Specifies evidence to preserve: SWIFT message/transaction logs (e.g., Alliance journals), authentication and access logs, network logs, and malware samples/images with chain of custody
expectations.evidence: "SWIFT application logs: Alliance Access/Entry logs, SWIFTNet Link logs, RMA logs, message audit trails", "Authentication/access logs: operator login records, HSM/token usage logs...", "Network logs: firewall, IDS/IPS, proxy, DNS, and netflow data", "Malware artifacts: samples of the malware binary/hashes...", and "Chain of custody documentation for all collected evidence"

expectations.text: Describes SWIFT-specific IR plan contents per Control 7.1: defined roles and contacts (including SWIFT and counterparties), SWIFT-zone isolation procedures, recovery/reconnection procedures, and periodic testing/exercises of the plan
expectations.evidence: "Roles and responsibilities — a named incident response team... plus 24/7 contact list", "a 'kill switch' procedure to disconnect from SWIFTNet if necessary", "validated clean rebuild process for SWIFT infrastructure, re-attestation of integrity before reconnecting to SWIFTNet", and "the plan must be tested at least annually (tabletop exercises or simulations)"
